content.problemStatement:
Today is Devu's birthday. He has obtained few colored balloons from his friends. You are given this information by a string **s** consisting of lower case English Latin letters. Each letter (from 'a' to 'z')  denotes a color. e.g. if s = "aab", then it means that he has received two balloons of color 'a' whereas one balloon of color 'b'.


Now, Devu wants to decorate the cake by arranging all the balloons linearly from left to right on the cake such that no same colored balloons are nearby/ adjacent to each other.



Now Devu wonders whether it is possible to do so or not? Please help him in this. If it is not possible to do so, print -1. Otherwise, print any one of arrangements of the balloons on the cake. If there are more than one possible ways of doing so, you can print any one of them.
constraints: Sum of size of string s over all test cases will be less than or equal to ≤ 10^6
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains an integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. First line of each test case will contain string s
content.outputFormat: Print a single line corresponding to the answer of the problem. If it is not possible to arrange the balloons, print -1.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1. He can arrange the balloons in the order given by the following string "aba". 
Example case 2. He can arrange the balloons in the order given by the following string "ab"
Example case 3. There is no valid way of decorating cakes with balloon in the desired way. So we print -1.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a greedy algorithm. We will count the frequency of each color and then try to arrange them in a way that no two balloons of the same color are adjacent. We can use a priority queue to store the colors and their frequencies. We will always pop the color with the highest frequency from the queue and append it to the result string. If the last character of the result string is the same as the popped color, we will try to pop another color from the queue and append it to the result string. If the queue is empty or the last character of the result string is the same as the popped color, we will return -1.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * n log n) — where n is the length of the string s. The reason is that we are using a priority queue to store the colors and their frequencies, and we are popping and pushing elements from the queue in each iteration.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n) — where n is the length of the string s. The reason is that we are using a priority queue to store the colors and their frequencies, and in the worst case, the size of the queue can be n.
